title: Serengeti 2-Day Wildlife Safari
metaTitle: Serengeti 2-Day Wildlife Safari | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 2-day trip to Serengeti, Tanzania: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Serengeti National Park entry and game drives and Hot air balloon safari at dawn. Prices from $1760 per person (estimate).
highlights: Serengeti National Park entry and game drives
Hot air balloon safari at dawn
Private-guide fly-in safari with a premium tented camp
overview: Spend 2 days discovering Serengeti, Tanzania — Warm savanna climate with two rainy seasons (November-December short rains, March-May long rains); daytime highs stay around 25-28°C year-round. June to October for the dry season and Western Corridor river crossings; December to March to witness the wildebeest calving season in the southern plains. This itinerary blends Serengeti National Park entry and game drives, Hot air balloon safari at dawn, Camp/lodge wildlife viewing with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
1 night accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Serengeti National Park entry and game drives, Hot air balloon safari at dawn, Camp/lodge wildlife viewing
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Serengeti
itinerary.description: Land at Kilimanjaro International Airport (main gateway; onward light-aircraft transfer to Serengeti airstrips) (JRO) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. With limited time on the ground, head straight out to Serengeti National Park entry and game drives — Vast plains hosting the Great Migration and dense predator populations. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.
activities: Serengeti National Park entry and game drives

itinerary.title: Serengeti Highlights & Departure
itinerary.description: Start early with a visit to Hot air balloon safari at dawn — Sunrise flight over the plains followed by a champagne bush breakfast. Grab a last taste of local life before your private transfer to JRO for your onward flight.
activities: Hot air balloon safari at dawn

travelTips: Book migration-season safaris (especially river crossings) 6-12 months in advance — camps sell out.
A hot air balloon safari at dawn is the single most memorable optional add-on most travelers choose.
Combine the Serengeti with the Ngorongoro Crater for a very different, more concentrated wildlife experience.
Follow your guide's vehicle-exit rules strictly — this is genuinely dangerous wildlife territory.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Neutral safari colors (khaki, olive) — avoid bright colors and dark blue/black which attract tsetse flies
Warm layers for cold early-morning game drives
Malaria prophylaxis as advised by a doctor
thingsToDo: Serengeti National Park entry and game drives
Hot air balloon safari at dawn
Walking safari with an armed ranger (select conservancies)
Multi-day mobile camping safari following the migration
Family-friendly tented camps with junior ranger programs
Game drives suited to older children
thingsToAvoid: Booking during the wrong migration window for your priority sighting — research crossing timing carefully.
Underestimating flight/drive logistics between Arusha and the various Serengeti zones.
Skipping travel and medical evacuation insurance for a remote safari.
topRestaurants: Camp/lodge dining (all-inclusive) — International and East African, bush-camp style ($$$$)
shoppingAreas: Curio shops at lodges and the Ngorongoro Crater rim
nightlife: Campfire evenings with guides sharing bush stories
instagramSpots: Hot air balloon over the plains at sunrise
Big cats on a kopje rock outcrop
hiddenGems: Western Corridor during the migration's river-crossing window
familyActivities: Family-friendly tented camps with junior ranger programs
Game drives suited to older children
adventureActivities: Walking safari with an armed ranger (select conservancies)
Multi-day mobile camping safari following the migration
luxuryExperiences: Private-guide fly-in safari with a premium tented camp
Hot air balloon safari with champagne breakfast
Exclusive-use villa camp with a dedicated vehicle
honeymoonExperiences: Star-bed night under the open sky at select camps
Champagne breakfast after a hot air balloon flight

festivalCalendar.name: Great Migration river crossings (natural event, not a festival)

localEtiquette: Follow your guide's instructions at all times during game drives — never exit the vehicle outside designated areas.
Keep noise low during wildlife sightings.
Tipping guides and camp staff (15-20 USD per day) is standard practice.
localTransportGuide: Access is by light aircraft to a bush airstrip or by 4x4 safari vehicle from Arusha, typically arranged entirely through a tour operator. There is no independent public transport within the park — all movement happens with a licensed guide and vehicle as part of a booked safari.
bestTimeToVisit: June to October for the dry season and Western Corridor river crossings; December to March to witness the wildebeest calving season in the southern plains.
weather: Warm savanna climate with two rainy seasons (November-December short rains, March-May long rains); daytime highs stay around 25-28°C year-round.
visaInformation: Tanzania offers an e-visa or visa-on-arrival for most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India — arranging the e-visa in advance is recommended to avoid arrival queues.
averageFlightTime: ~11.4h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
nearestAirport: Kilimanjaro International Airport (main gateway; onward light-aircraft transfer to Serengeti airstrips) (JRO)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Serengeti?
faqs.answer: Tanzania offers an e-visa or visa-on-arrival for most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India — arranging the e-visa in advance is recommended to avoid arrival queues.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Serengeti?
faqs.answer: June to October for the dry season and Western Corridor river crossings; December to March to witness the wildebeest calving season in the southern plains. Warm savanna climate with two rainy seasons (November-December short rains, March-May long rains); daytime highs stay around 25-28°C year-round.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Serengeti safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Serengeti is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Serengeti?
faqs.answer: The local currency is TZS. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Isabella Rossi
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Ngorongoro Crater day excursion and Camp/lodge wildlife viewing. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Fatima Tanaka
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Camp/lodge wildlife viewing alone was worth the trip, but combined with Hot air balloon safari at dawn this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Arjun Sharma
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Ngorongoro Crater day excursion in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Mia Tanaka
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Hot air balloon safari at dawn and Hot air balloon safari at dawn. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Our trip to Serengeti was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Serengeti National Park entry and game drives was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.
